mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning in equity markets, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points within 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and housing starts indicates synchronized demand and supply shocks that overwhelm corporate pricing power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 1.5% compared to a single-proxy baseline, or forward returns show no significant difference when both series decline simultaneously versus when only one declines.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, rising policy rates increase borrowing costs for leveraged corporations, compressing profit margins and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero during documented tightening cycles, or the coefficient on FEDFUNDS loses significance (p > 0.10) in a regime-filtered regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real personal consumption expenditures reflect expanding aggregate demand that directly boosts corporate revenue growth expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-month Spearman correlation between RSAFS momentum and 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.10, or forward returns are negative in the top quartile of RSAFS acceleration periods.

mechanism_narrative: A breakdown in industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals supply chain disruptions that raise input costs and depress margins, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite sustained INDPRO declines below the moving average, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for concurrent CPIAUCSL spikes.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous decline in nonfarm payrolls growth and real personal consumption expenditures indicates a self-reinforcing demand-labor feedback loop that contracts economic activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy model in out-of-sample directional accuracy, or forward returns show no significant divergence when both series weaken versus when only one weakens.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, rapid rebounds in housing starts amplify consumer wealth effects and construction-related corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: HOUST rebounds fail to translate into positive equity returns, or the forward return differential between recovery and non-recovery periods is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).
